Hello! How can I assist you today?

convert into english char and make it meaningful सलमान खान, शाहरुख खान हम लोग कर रहे हैं करण अर्जुन कहा उसमें सलमान खान करता था फ्री! वो कोई टेंशन नहीं हूं। मस्त मलंग एक अजीब कैरेक्टर अपनी दुनिया में अब भी दिल में बैठ गया। चलना गया। शाहरुख खान। डिटेल में घुसता था। मेरा रोल है, पिछला जनम है, फलाना है तो मैं ऐसा वैसा ही। सलमान में पिछले जनम तो ठीक है मालूम नहीं मेरे पता ठीक है। पिछले जन्म में वो था, ये था, ठीक है और अंदर घुसा था। इनके रीटेक हो जाता था। अच्छा फिर कर रहे हैं, फिर कर रहे हैं, फिर कर रहे हैं। शूटिंग के बाद फिर होटल में जाकर मैं क्या करूं? या मैं क्या करूं या मैं क्या करूं? तो सलमान करके निकल जाऊं तो लगा भाई। पहले बड़ा स्टार बनाया बड़ा स्टार बन गया था। इसीलिए सिर्फ सलमान फिरकी लेता था। मजाक करता था। मजाक उड़ा था शाहरुख खान का। वह आदमी करके अपने आपको बनाया। फिर से डांस म्यूजिक बढ़िया बन गए। फाइट बढ़िया बन।

It appears that the provided text is in Hindi and refers to Indian Bollywood actors Salman Khan and Shah Rukh Khan, possibly discussing anecdotes related to their work and experiences. The text appears informal and conversational, and could be discussing their approaches to acting, past roles, or possibly the making of a film they both worked on, possibly referencing the 1995 Indian film "Karan Arjun," in which both actors starred.

Here is a rough translation of the text into English, aimed at capturing the context and preserving the conversational tone:

---

Salman Khan, Shah Rukh Khan, we are doing [or talking about] "Karan Arjun." Salman Khan would do scenes for free in that! That's not worrying at all. A carefree, unique character who has settled in our hearts even now. Kept going. Shah Rukh Khan used to get into the details. It's my role, my past life, this or that, then I behave accordingly. With Salman, it's okay not to know about his past life. My address is correct. In his past life, he was this or that, that's right, and he got into it. He would call for retakes. Okay, then doing it again, and again, and again. After shooting, what do I do back at the hotel? What do I do, what do I do? So, after Salman does it, it feels right, brother. First, he became a big star, then he really became a big star. That's why only Salman would tease. He would joke around. He made fun of Shah Rukh Khan. That man created himself. Then again, the dance and music got great. The fight scenes became top-notch.

---

Please note that dialogues or spoken text can often include cultural nuances or slang that might not translate directly. Additionally, the text seems to be fragmented and might be a part of a larger conversation or speech, which makes it challenging to interpret and convey in English without additional context.

The text you've provided is somewhat jumbled and seems to be an amalgamation of thoughts or spoken words by someone discussing the Bollywood actors Salman Khan and Shah Rukh Khan. It touches on anecdotes or possibly shared experiences and serves as a commentary on their personas and working styles. Here's an attempt to coalesce the provided Hindi text into coherent and meaningful English:

"Salman Khan and Shah Rukh Khan - we were doing 'Karan Arjun,' where Salman Khan worked for free! He is not worried at all. The lively 'malang' (a term that can mean a carefree person or someone lost in love/devotion) is a strange character who has made a place in his own world and is still seated in our hearts. Shah Rukh Khan dives into details. He has his role, his past life, and all such things, so he behaves accordingly. With Salman, it's okay; his past life isn't clear; I don't know, my address is correct. In his past life, he was this and that; that's fine; he was immersed in it. Retakes would be needed for his scenes. Okay, then doing it again, and again, and again. After shooting, what should I do when I go back to the hotel? What should I do, what should I do? So, if I do it like Salman and finish it off, it feels right, brother. First, he made himself a big star, and he really became a big star. That’s why Salman used to tease. He used to joke around. He made fun of Shah Rukh Khan. That man became what he is on his own. The dance and music became good. The fight scenes became great."

The provided text is a mix of Hindi and transliterated English, and it seems to be a conversational excerpt about the actors Salman Khan and Shah Rukh Khan. It appears to discuss their working styles and their roles in the film "Karan Arjun," including some light-hearted teasing between the two actors. Below is an attempt to provide a coherent translation interspersed with transliterated terms:

---

"Salman Khan and Shah Rukh Khan, we were doing 'Karan Arjun', where Salman Khan used to take his shots for free! He doesn't take any tension. The carefree malang (spiritually free person), a unique character in his own right, absolutely at ease in his world. Shah Rukh Khan, he used to delve into details. 'This is my role, my past life, this or that,' so I am just like that. Salman used to say, 'Knowing about the past life is okay, but I know my address.' In his past life, he was this or that, okay, and Shah Rukh would immerse himself deeply. Because of him, retakes would happen. Okay, so then doing again, and again, and again. After shooting, what should I do when I return to the hotel? What should I do, what should I do? So once Salman wraps up, I feel I should remain as well, brother. Salman used to tell Shah Rukh that you have become a big star, you are a big star now. Salman used to tease Shah Rukh, joke with him. He used to make fun of Shah Rukh Khan. That man (Shah Rukh) made a name for himself with his efforts. Then again, the dance and music turned out great. The fight scenes turned out great."

---

This translation captures the gist of the conversation, but the nuances of humor and camaraderie that are present in the original Hindi may not completely come through in English. The text mentions "mast malang," "retakes," and "firki lete tha," which are colloquial in nature and may denote the easy-going nature of one actor and the meticulousness of the other. The discussion around "pichhla janam" (past life) seems to refer to the details of the characters they played, or could be a metaphor for their acting careers and philosophies. The text clearly conveys a sense of fondness and jest between the two actors.
